nodes.body: <<FILL role=setup words=124 beats='first morning of summer and the old Ranger lodge at Alderbrook Camp finally opens its tall green doors to you, camp registration card in hand; inside smells of brass polish, sawdust, and glue; Camp Director Nora Finch fits you with your own safety glasses and points out three workshop doors off the great hall, one ringed with carved wooden trail markers, one strung with faded canoe pennants, one lined with little glass terrariums and cages opening onto Trading Post Lane'>>

choices.label: Follow Camp Director Nora Finch on the tour.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=112 beats='the tour winds through all three workshops: in the gear hall Ranger Tomas Vega polishes a model of the town square's silent four-season fountain clock; up on the roof Coach Marisela Reyes clips your harness to the launch dock and shows off the glider launch ramp and the pigeon den; in the gadget shop Nurse Aiyana Cole is mending a shopkeeper's bell and makes you ask the question twice; every mentor says the same thing, tomorrow is the entrance the badge trial'>>

choices.label: Take the the badge trial.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=116 beats='the the badge trial: a box of odd parts, springs, dowels, a cracked pulley, one bent hinge, and one hour to make something that does something; your contraption wobbles, jams, gets rebuilt twice, and finally whirs across the table to polite cheers; Camp Director Nora Finch, you notice, was not watching what you built, she was watching how you fixed it each time it failed'>>

choices.label: Hear Camp Director Nora Finch's verdict.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=118 beats='Camp Director Nora Finch says the test proves you could thrive behind any of the three doors, so the choice is yours for the whole summer: the Trailcraft workshop, under Ranger Tomas Vega, is restoring the camp's old trail-marker network for the Founders' Trail Day; the Riverwatch workshop, under Coach Marisela Reyes, is training for the river regatta races; the Critter Care workshop, under Nurse Aiyana Cole, is caring for injured local wildlife before the Camp Showcase; three doors stand open'>>

choices.label: Join the Trailcraft workshop.
Join the Riverwatch workshop.
Join the Critter Care workshop.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='your first morning in the gear hall: a workbench with your name chalked on it, rows of tiny screwdrivers, and Ranger Tomas Vega peering through her magnifier glasses at you the way she inspects a gear, kindly but closely; safety glasses on before anything turns, she says'>>

choices.label: Settle in at your bench.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='Ranger Tomas Vega unveils the guild's model of the fountain clock: on every hour, four brass season figures, a spring lark carving, a summer badger carving, an autumn marten carving, a winter wren carving, should parade around the fountain rim while eight bells chime, all driven by a old water pump wheel under the square; the real clock has stood silent for twenty years, and the guild has promised the town it will run again at the Founders' Trail Day'>>

choices.label: Ask where you come in.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='Devon, a junior with two summers of grease under his nails, announces that the hard jobs are his and the toothbrush jobs are yours; Ranger Tomas Vega ignores this and splits the restoration in two: someone must wake the four season figures, and someone must take on the trail chime posts and the old water pump wheel below the square'>>

choices.label: Hear the two jobs out.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='Ranger Tomas Vega lays two work orders on your bench and lets you choose your summer: the season figures wait in the workshop, green with age, a puzzle of tiny springs and fused wings; the trail chime posts means the clock tower stairs and the flooded old water pump wheel cellar, ropes, ladders, and bells bigger than you are; Devon mutters that he wants whichever one you do not take'>>

choices.label: Restore the four season figures.
Take on the trail chime posts and old water pump wheel.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the four figures stand on your bench like a tiny frozen parade: the spring lark carving's wings jammed mid-beat, the summer badger carving caught mid-leap, the autumn marten carving hunched, the winter wren carving's head locked staring backward; sixty years of grime hide every screw'>>

choices.label: Start with the spring lark carving.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the spring lark carving's wing gears are fused into one green lump, and Devon leans over your shoulder to say you will strip the teeth if you force it; annoyingly, he is right, your first try barely avoids disaster'>>

choices.label: Ask Ranger Tomas Vega for a better way.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Ranger Tomas Vega teaches you the slow cure: a vinegar bath, a soft toothbrush, and patience by the hour, and by the end of the week the spring lark carving's wing gives one clean tick and half a wingbeat; two figures remain in real trouble, and Ranger Tomas Vega says each is a different kind of hard'>>

choices.label: Look at the two worst figures.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='the summer badger carving's great leap spring is snapped clean in two, which means forging and coiling a new one at the forge corner, hot work you have never done; the winter wren carving's head is seized solid on a bent arbor deep inside its body, which means a careful teardown and a press; Ranger Tomas Vega will let you lead on exactly one'>>

choices.label: Reforge the summer badger carving's snapped leap spring.
Free the winter wren carving's seized head.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the forge corner smells of coal and hot metal; Ranger Tomas Vega shows you spring steel, how it must be coiled while cherry-red and cooled just so; Devon, passing, says spring coiling took him a whole summer to learn'>>

choices.label: Coil your first spring.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='your first coil snaps like a biscuit, the second comes out lopsided, the third looks almost right; your wrist aches and the festival is getting closer on the wall calendar; the last step is tempering, and Ranger Tomas Vega says that is where springs are truly made or ruined'>>

choices.label: Decide how to temper it.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='tempering is a choice between speed and certainty: quench the spring fast tonight and you could test the summer badger carving's leap tomorrow and still catch up on the other figures; or bank the coals and temper it slow overnight the way the old manuals say, spending days you barely have; Devon has left, the forge is yours'>>

choices.label: Quench the spring fast and test it tomorrow.
Temper it slowly overnight, the old way.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the fast-quenched spring looks perfect, bright and eager, and the summer badger carving takes one glorious test leap across the bench before a sharp ping says everything; the spring has shattered into glittering pieces, brittle from the hurry'>>

choices.label: Face Ranger Tomas Vega with the pieces.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the slow temper works: the new spring rings true when you tap it, and the summer badger carving leaps; but one good spring leads to the autumn marten carving's tired springs, then the spring lark carving's, and the summer quietly becomes a summer of springs'>>

choices.label: Follow where the springs lead.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the winter wren carving comes apart screw by careful screw until you find the trouble: the arbor its head turns on is bent like a hairpin, and it will not come free without an arbor press; the guild owns exactly one'>>

choices.label: Find out who has the press.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the press lives on Devon's bench, and Devon names his price with a grin: help him finish the autumn marten carving he is secretly behind on, or figure out your own way to pull a bent arbor; Ranger Tomas Vega, suspiciously busy nearby, says nothing at all'>>

choices.label: Weigh Devon's trade.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='you sketch a makeshift press out of a bench vise, two clamps, and stubbornness, and it might just work if nothing slips; or you could take Devon's deal, share the press, and spend your evenings on his autumn marten carving, which means trusting the one person who calls your work toothbrush jobs; the winter wren carving's backward stare is not helping'>>

choices.label: Rig your own press from the bench vise.
Take Devon's trade and share the press.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='the vise-press holds, creaks, holds, and then slips exactly the way Devon said it would; the arbor jumps like a snapped twig and a crack runs through the winter wren carving's crown gear, the one part nobody has a spare for'>>

choices.label: Show Ranger Tomas Vega the damage.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Ranger Tomas Vega turns the cracked crown gear in the light for a long minute; a new one must be cast in the city, she says, and casting takes past the Founders' Trail Day; some jobs, she adds gently, are two-person jobs no matter how clever one person is'>>

choices.label: Finish the summer without the winter wren carving.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='working with Devon turns out to be nothing like arguing with him: at the press he is careful, quick, and quietly funny, and the bent arbor slides free on the second pull; in trade you tune his autumn marten carving's hop until it lands like a feather'>>

choices.label: Wake the winter wren carving.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the winter wren carving's head turns, the autumn marten carving hops, the spring lark carving beats both wings, and the summer badger carving leaps on Devon's borrowed spring; then the basin test ruins everything: down at the fountain, the parade ring the figures ride on jams solid the moment the water flows over it'>>

choices.label: Study the jammed parade ring.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='a night of cocoa and sketches at Ranger Tomas Vega's big table turns up the answer in the oldest drawing: the parade ring was never meant to run underwater at all; there was once a spillway gate that lowered the basin for every parade, and it has been lost for decades'>>

choices.label: Bring the old drawing to Ranger Tomas Vega.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='Ranger Tomas Vega sees two honest paths to the Founders' Trail Day: run the parade with the basin drained dry, a sure thing the town would still love, and hunt the spillway gate at leisure afterward; or gamble the remaining weeks on finding and freeing the lost spillway gate so the seasons can parade over rushing water the way the makers meant; Devon says he is in either way'>>

choices.label: Run the parade dry, a sure thing.
Hunt the lost spillway gate with Devon.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='you drain the basin, true up the dry ring, and rehearse the parade until all four figures run like sunrise; Ranger Tomas Vega schedules a public dry-run reveal a week before the Founders' Trail Day, just to be safe'>>

choices.label: Hold the dry-run reveal.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='half the town shows up to the so-called small reveal; when the spring lark carving leads the summer badger carving, autumn marten carving, and winter wren carving around the dry rim for the first time in twenty years, the square goes quiet in the good way'>>

choices.label: Take your bow.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the guild archives smell of dust and secrets; you and Devon find the gatekeeper's diagram folded inside a ledger from before your grandparents: the drain crank hides behind the fountain's little brass door, the one everyone always assumed was decoration'>>

choices.label: Open the brass door.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='behind the brass door, under forty years of mud, the drain crank still turns; you clean it tooth by tooth while Devon rebuilds its chain, and at dawn on a Tuesday the basin empties in ninety glorious seconds, then fills again like magic'>>

choices.label: Rehearse for the Founders' Trail Day.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the Founders' Trail Day morning: bunting on every lamppost, the square packed shoulder to shoulder, the basin brimming; Ranger Tomas Vega holds out the great trailhead bell rope, which by guild custom belongs to the restorer's hand, while Devon waits at the brass door ready on the drain crank'>>

choices.label: Step up to the clock.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='one minute to noon and one honor to give away: take the trailhead bell rope Ranger Tomas Vega is offering and set the seasons parading yourself while Devon works the hidden crank; or press the trailhead bell rope into Devon's hand, two summers earned, and take the crank behind the brass door, the job nobody sees and nothing works without'>>

choices.label: Wind the clock yourself, as Ranger Tomas Vega offered.
Give Devon the trailhead bell rope and take the hidden crank.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='you set the trailhead bell rope, brace, and wind; underground the drain thunders open on Devon's crank, the water bows out of the basin, and the four seasons step out over the wet-shining ring exactly at noon'>>

choices.label: Watch the seasons run.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='Devon stares at the trailhead bell rope in his hand like it might be a joke, then grips it like it is treasure; you duck behind the brass door, hands on the crank, watching noon come through a slot the size of a letterbox'>>

choices.label: Turn the crank at noon.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the clock tower stairs corkscrew up past the great silent bells, eight of them, each cast with a season vine; far below, a dripping cellar under the square holds the old water pump wheel, drowned in sixty years of dead leaves; Ranger Tomas Vega gives you the tower key and one rule: never past the yellow rail without a rope-buddy'>>

choices.label: Survey your new kingdom.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the survey is honest and grim: the old water pump wheel below is so clogged it cannot turn a spoon, and the trail chime posts above has a bent hammer, slipped cams, and one bell that only whispers; nothing rings and nothing turns, and the Founders' Trail Day is eight weeks out'>>

choices.label: Report to Ranger Tomas Vega.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Devon appears at the tower door with a bet: he can voice the bells sweeter than you can, any day; Ranger Tomas Vega turns the bet into a plan, Devon may race you on bell voicing, but the timekeeping is yours alone, because a chime that rings sweet but late, she says, is just a pretty lie'>>

choices.label: Choose where to start.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='two starts, one choice: begin below in the cellar, freeing the old water pump wheel that powers everything, cold muddy work that makes every later job possible; or begin above with the trail chime posts itself, mapping cams and hammers in the high bell chamber while Devon tunes and hums across the scaffold from you'>>

choices.label: Start below and free the old water pump wheel.
Start above and rebuild the trail chime posts.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the cellar old water pump wheel is taller than a door, furred with leaf-rot, and stuck fast; you bail, rake, and scrape for days, and when the axle finally groans a quarter-turn you cheer alone in the dark like it was the Founders' Trail Day already'>>

choices.label: Inspect the freed old water pump wheel.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='freed is not fixed: three paddles are rotten right through, and the sluice that feeds the old water pump wheel will pound whatever you mount there; Ranger Tomas Vega is up a scaffold all week, Devon is bell-deep in his bet, and the festival calendar keeps losing days; the paddle question is yours to settle'>>

choices.label: Settle the paddle question.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='the quick way: patch the rotten paddles with tin sheet and clever folding, done in two days, probably fine, probably; the long way: fell, split, and carve proper oak paddles like the millwrights did, most of a summer's work for a old water pump wheel nobody will ever see; the sluice drips while you think'>>

choices.label: Patch the paddles quickly with tin.
Carve proper oak paddles all summer.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the tin patches gleam and the old water pump wheel turns a full week, long enough to feel like a winner, and then the sluice pounds one patch loose, then another, and the shed tin swims down and chokes the grating on the very morning of the full test'>>

choices.label: Pull the choked grating.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='oak is stubborn and honest: all summer you learn to split it true, shape it with the drawknife, and set each paddle so the sluice water lands flat and full; the bells wait, the bet goes to Devon by default, and your hands grow carpenter-hard'>>

choices.label: Mount the last paddle.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the bell chamber becomes your map room: every cam chalk-numbered, every hammer measured, ropes coiled and tagged; Devon voices bells across the scaffold, and his third bell rings so sweet you both stop working just to listen to it die away'>>

choices.label: Trace the late chimes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the missing quarter-bell cam turns up in a biscuit tin, of course, but the real trouble hides lower: Devon's bells ring beautifully and always four beats late, and the fault is neither bells nor cams but the escapement between old water pump wheel and chime, the clock's heartbeat itself'>>

choices.label: Open the escapement housing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='inside the housing you find a forty-year-old bodge: someone replaced the compass gimbal with a filed-down wrong part that has been limping ever since; a true compass gimbal must be made new, and compass gimbal-making, Ranger Tomas Vega says quietly, is master-level filing she has watched grown inventors fail'>>

choices.label: Decide how the compass gimbal gets made.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='the castings cupboard holds three rough compass gimbal blanks, and you have evenings and stubbornness enough to file one secretly and surprise everyone, if your hands are as good as you hope; or you can ask Ranger Tomas Vega to teach you compass gimbal-filing properly, three slow weeks of lessons in front of everyone, with Devon watching every stroke'>>

choices.label: File an compass gimbal alone, working nights.
Ask Ranger Tomas Vega to teach you, three slow weeks.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='night filing goes wrong the way tired hands always do: the first blank goes under-square on Tuesday, the second chips on Thursday, and past midnight on Friday Ranger Tomas Vega finds you asleep at the bench, cheek on the third and last blank'>>

choices.label: Wake up to Ranger Tomas Vega's lantern.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='Ranger Tomas Vega does not scold; she puts the last blank back in the cupboard, walks you home under streetlights, and says the strongest thing an inventor ever makes is the sentence I need help; the compass gimbal will wait for autumn lessons'>>

choices.label: Keep time the old way.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='three weeks of lessons: file, breathe, check the square, file again; Devon drifts over nightly to watch and stays to learn, trading his bell-voicing tricks for your filing strokes, until Ranger Tomas Vega starts calling the pair of you the tower crew like it is one word'>>

choices.label: Finish the true compass gimbal.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the finished compass gimbal drops into the escapement like it grew there, and the clock breathes: tick, tock, steady as a sleeping cat, the first true beat in twenty years; Devon's bells come in four beats early now, which is to say, exactly on time'>>

choices.label: Rehearse the full chime.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='festival eve, and Ranger Tomas Vega reveals what the Founders' Trail Day actually is: at noon one inventor winds the great weight, one voices the bells, and one keeps the old water pump wheel, three posts held once in a lifetime; the guild asks the tower crew how tomorrow's noon will be crewed, and leaves the answer to you'>>

choices.label: Answer at the noon rehearsal.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='three ways to stand at the first true noon: take the winding post yourself with Devon on bells, the compass gimbal-maker setting her own heartbeat going; hand Devon the winding and conduct the whole hour from the tower rail, timing bells, old water pump wheel, and seasons together; or slip your untested quarter-chime surprise, finished last night, into the noon program and make history twice'>>

choices.label: Take the winding post, Devon on bells.
Give Devon the winding and conduct the hour.
Add your untested quarter-chime surprise.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='you set your palms to the great trailhead bell rope as the square fills; below, the old water pump wheel takes the water, above, Devon stands ready at his sweetest bell, and the compass gimbal, your compass gimbal, waits to measure out the town's first honest noon in twenty years'>>

choices.label: Wind, and let noon come.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='Devon takes the winding post with shaking hands and a grin you have never seen on him; you climb to the tower rail with the pocket watch and the cue sheet, one eye on the old water pump wheel gauge, one on the bells, the whole clock laid out below you like an orchestra'>>

choices.label: Conduct the first noon.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='your quarter-chime surprise, a ninth hammer rigged overnight to trill between the hours, goes into the noon program on a hope and a wire; there was no time to test it, and standing at the cam you can feel exactly how much no time there was'>>

choices.label: Ring the surprise at noon.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=112 beats='Ranger Tomas Vega sweeps the glitter into an envelope and labels it lesson one: hurry is hard on steel; there is no time left to forge another before the Founders' Trail Day, so the summer badger carving watches the festival from the workshop window while the other three figures parade without it; on the last day of summer Ranger Tomas Vega hands you the envelope and a winter invitation to the forge, because spring-makers, she says, are made of exactly this kind of mistake'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=116 beats='by the Founders' Trail Day you have not seen the tower or the old water pump wheel cellar even once, but every spring in all four figures is yours, and when the parade finally runs on festival day each leap and wingbeat lands true; Ranger Tomas Vega introduces you to the crowd as the guild's new keeper of springs, a title nobody planned and nobody else earned; you came to fix a clock and instead discovered the one small thing you may be best at in the whole guild'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=114 beats='three seasons parade at the Founders' Trail Day while winter rides in a padded crate to the city foundry; the crowd cheers anyway, but you know the gap in the parade by heart; Devon finds you afterward and says, not unkindly, that he cracked a crown gear his first summer too, which is why he bought the press; you spend your last week drafting the winter wren carving's rebuild plan for autumn, and Ranger Tomas Vega signs it approved with her red pencil'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=118 beats='the four seasons parade every hour on the Founders' Trail Day day, dry basin and all, and nobody under ten cares even slightly about the missing water; Ranger Tomas Vega announces the spillway gate hunt as next summer's first project and posts the sign-up sheet with your name already at the top, Devon's second; twenty years of silence, she tells the crowd, ended because two juniors learned to share one press; the summer badger carving leaps on the hour, every hour, all summer long'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=120 beats='the spring lark carving, summer badger carving, autumn marten carving, and winter wren carving parade over rushing, returning water while eight bells ring and the whole square sings the Rewinding song; Ranger Tomas Vega engraves two names on the maintenance plate inside the pedestal, yours and Devon's, where only future menders will ever read them, which she says is exactly the right audience; you started the summer as the toothbrush kid and ended it as the junior who woke the seasons'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=118 beats='from inside the pedestal the parade is all sound: the drain roaring at your crank, gears taking up, the crowd gasping somewhere above, Devon laughing like a much younger kid; afterward he cannot stop saying it should have been you, and you cannot explain that it was, that turning the hidden crank taught you what Ranger Tomas Vega meant all summer: half of every machine, and most of every guild, is the part that works out of sight'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=114 beats='the verdict is nobody's favorite: the old water pump wheel must come out for a full rebuild come autumn, and the Founders' Trail Day runs on the emergency hand-wind, juniors taking turns at a crank in the cellar while the bells ring above; your turn comes at noon exactly, which Ranger Tomas Vega calls fitting; you spend the last weeks drawing the rebuild plan, tin envelope pinned to the corner as a warning, and the guild votes it the best-documented failure of the year'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=116 beats='on the last day of summer the old water pump wheel turns true under full sluice for the first time in sixty years, so smooth you can balance a coin on the frame, and it will turn that way for sixty more; the town miller comes down to watch, runs his palm along your paddles, and offers you winter work at the mill on the spot; you never rang a bell all summer, and somehow found the trade your hands were built for'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=112 beats='the Founders' Trail Day gets its chimes anyway, hand-rung: you in the bell chamber with a mallet, a pocket watch, and Devon calling the beats, ringing noon so nearly perfect that only the two of you know the clock itself stayed silent; the town hears bells for the first time in twenty years and dances; you hear the four-beat gap where your compass gimbal should have been, and sign up for autumn filing lessons before the last bell fades'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=122 beats='noon arrives through your own hands: the weight rises, the compass gimbal beats, eight bells ring in perfect step while the four seasons parade below and the whole town sings the Rewinding song louder than the chimes; Ranger Tomas Vega engraves the tower crew's names on the maintenance plate and declares the clock alive for another lifetime; on the way down Devon asks what you will restore next summer, and the wonderful truth is you have absolutely no idea'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=118 beats='your countdown brings old water pump wheel, weight, bells, and seasons in together like sections of a band, and noon rings so exactly true that the station master checks his railway watch against the tower instead of the other way round; Devon shouts your name from the winding post for the whole square to hear; timing the team, Ranger Tomas Vega says over cocoa, is timekeeping, and she should know, she has been doing it to the pair of you all summer'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=116 beats='the surprise cam skips at the worst moment, tangles the quarter-bell rope, and noon limps in four minutes late while you untie knots sixty feet above the laughing, kindly crowd; Ranger Tomas Vega's verdict is one sentence, test before showtime, pinned above your bench forever; you post the fix by nightfall, ring a perfect private noon at midnight for an audience of Devon and two pigeons, and the guild adds your honest fix-note to its book of famous four-minute mistakes'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the spiral stair ends in wind: the rooftop aero workshop is a long shed of balsa smell and wing ribs, wind socks snapping on every corner; Coach Marisela Reyes, an old airship rigger with weather-carved hands, clips a harness line to your belt before hello and teaches you the roof's first law, clip before you step'>>

choices.label: Clip in and look around.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Coach Marisela Reyes walks you along the parapet and points out the derby course: a launch dock on the guild roof, three hoops rigged over three rooftops, and the landing lawn far below in the park; at summer's end the River Regatta decides the year's best flier, and your bench inherits a half-wrecked practice canoe with someone else's initials on the keel'>>

choices.label: Meet the rest of the roof.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='a grey blur drops out of the sun and lands on the den rail: Torrent, champion message pigeon, seven summers unbeaten, followed by his person, Callie Boone, who reads your face and decides you are not competition yet; Coach Marisela Reyes says the roof teaches two crafts, wings you build and wings you train, and every junior must pick a first love'>>

choices.label: Pick your first love.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='two summer roads leave this rooftop: rebuild the battered trainer rib by rib until it can fly the derby course, a builder's road of glue, clamps, and test hops; or join the pigeon post, take on a bird of your own, and fly the post routes against Callie Boone and Torrent all summer, a trainer's road of dawn launches and weather; Callie Boone is pretending not to listen'>>

choices.label: Rebuild the practice canoe for the River Regatta.
Join the pigeon post and race Torrent.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='you strip the practice canoe to its keel like a fish to the bone, numbering every rib with a stub of pencil; under the torn skin the old girl is honest wood, and Coach Marisela Reyes nods over your shoulder saying she flew the derby twice before either of you was born'>>

choices.label: Test what the old wing knows.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the wind tunnel is a crate, a borrowed box fan, and wool streamers taped to the wing, and it tells the truth anyway: the streamers dance smooth along the middle and snarl into knots at both wingtips; the old wing stalls at the tips, which Coach Marisela Reyes says is what finally grounded her'>>

choices.label: Ask Coach Marisela Reyes about the tip stall.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='Coach Marisela Reyes answers the tip stall with two drawings pinned side by side, a washout twist built into both wingtips, and two honest derby philosophies: a long long touring canoe that rides the high line above the chimneys, slow, patient, and certain of the distance; or a short short sprint kayak for the fast line under the rooftop hoops, quick and twitchy and thrilling if you can hold it; the practice canoe's keel will take either, exactly once'>>

choices.label: Build the long long touring canoe for the high line.
Build the short short sprint kayak for the fast line.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='long wings need long spars, and long spars mean scarf joints, thin wedges of spruce glued and clamped overnight in a jig you build twice before it is straight; the shed smells of glue for a week and your fingertips wear little shells of it'>>

choices.label: Take the long touring canoe up for a hop.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='off the low shed roof the long touring canoe simply refuses to come down, floating the length of the guild in one breath, but she wanders like a daydream, drifting left of wherever you aim her; Coach Marisela Reyes diagnoses a missing rudder trim tab, a small part with strong opinions, and the derby trials are tomorrow'>>

choices.label: Face the trim tab problem.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='a wing this long wants two more hands at the launch dock: a tail-line crew to steady her through launch and a voice to call the drift at the hoops; but the derby trials paint only the pilot's name on the board, and asking Callie Boone to crew means handing your build's every secret to your rival a month before the derby; the trials fly tomorrow at dawn, with or without you deciding out loud'>>

choices.label: Fly the trials solo tomorrow, no crew.
Ask Callie Boone and a friend to crew the launch.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='solo at dawn, no one on the tail line and no voice calling the drift: the long touring canoe leaves the launch dock crooked, floats the first hoop anyway, then wanders wide of the second with your hands too full to correct, and settles herself, gentle and unbothered, into the market's striped awning like a cat into a hammock'>>

choices.label: Fetch her down from the awning.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='Callie Boone says yes before you finish asking, and drafts a junior from the gadget shop for gust duty; the first crewed launch leaves the launch dock straight as a ruled line, and with a voice on the tail rope calling the drift, the long touring canoe's famous wander shrinks to a whisper by the second morning'>>

choices.label: Fly the crewed month out.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the crewed month is the best flying of the summer: Callie Boone calls the drift from the tail line, the gadget shop junior times the gusts, and between launches the three of you tune the long touring canoe's trim tab until she flies a chalk line the length of the roof; other fliers start drifting over to ask about their own wandering wings'>>

choices.label: See where the crew work leads.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the short sprint kayak comes together short-coupled and eager, a wing that answers before you finish asking; on the bench she is perfect, but the fast line under the hoops leaves no room for a sloppy start, and Coach Marisela Reyes says everything about the short sprint kayak's derby will be decided by the launch dock release'>>

choices.label: Study the launch release.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the only release hook that never misfires on this roof is Callie Boone's own design, guild-registered with her name stamped in the brass; you could ask to borrow the pattern, which means asking Callie Boone for help, or puzzle out a copy from watching it work, which means not quite asking; Coach Marisela Reyes raises one eyebrow and leaves the ethics to you'>>

choices.label: Decide about the hook.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='the copy would be easy, that is the trouble: two evenings with calipers while Callie Boone flies Torrent, and no one the wiser unless it slips; asking her straight out costs pride you have been saving all summer, and she might just laugh; the short sprint kayak waits on the launch dock, hookless, while you decide who you are going to be about this'>>

choices.label: Rig a quiet copy of Callie Boone's hook.
Ask Callie Boone straight out to share the release.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='your copy is nearly right, which on a launch hook means wrong: at the trials it lets go a half-beat early, the short sprint kayak noses off the launch dock, and she plants herself in the straw bales below the parapet, splintered but whole, while the field flies on without you'>>

choices.label: Pull the short sprint kayak from the straw.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='Callie Boone appears while you are still picking straw off the wreck, arms crossed, and does not say what she could; she says her first hook misfired for a whole spring before she got it right, and that the real pattern is yours next season, all you ever had to do was ask'>>

choices.label: Shake on next season.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Callie Boone says yes so fast you suspect she was waiting to be asked, and names her price: evening laps helping train Torrent while she rebuilds her own entry; the true hook fits the short sprint kayak's launch dock like it grew there, and your launches go from lottery to clockwork in a week'>>

choices.label: Train evenings, fly mornings.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='mid-summer, a green-black storm tears across the town in an hour and takes the launch dock with it, peeling it off the parapet and dropping the flagpole through the den roof for good measure; no launch dock, no derby, and the guild goes quiet the way a roof does when the wind stops'>>

choices.label: Survey the wreckage with Coach Marisela Reyes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='in the wreck you see it: the fallen flagpole is straight-grained and true, long enough to build not one launch dock but two side by side, a twin-launch dock launch like the old airship slips used; Coach Marisela Reyes turns your sketch over twice and says the words every junior waits for, bold, and possible'>>

choices.label: Put the plan to the guild.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='the guild will back one rebuild before derby day: restore the single launch dock exactly as it was, safe, proven, and done with a week to spare for practice; or build your twin-launch dock from the storm's own flagpole, a faster launch than this roof has ever had, finished, if nothing slips, the night before the derby with no full test flight; Callie Boone votes with her eyebrows for the twin-launch dock'>>

choices.label: Rebuild the single launch dock, safe and proven.
Build the twin-launch dock from the flagpole.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='you choose the sure road and build it well: the single launch dock goes back straighter than the storm found it, and the saved week becomes practice, dawn after dawn through the hoops until the short sprint kayak's line is grooved into your hands'>>

choices.label: Fly the derby you trained for.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='derby day runs like your practice mornings with bunting on: launch, hoop, hoop, hoop, lawn, the short sprint kayak flying her line like water finding a channel while the crowd on the street below tracks her shadow between the buildings'>>

choices.label: Land and hear the scores.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the twin-launch dock build eats the roof: sawhorses, pole sections, Callie Boone running Torrent across town for hardware while shopkeepers gather in the street to watch the twin rails rise against the sky; Coach Marisela Reyes checks every bolt behind you, twice, and teaches you to like it'>>

choices.label: Bolt down the twin rails.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='dawn tests, goggles down: the twin-launch dock throws the short sprint kayak off the roof like a swift leaving a barn, faster than any launch this roof has seen, and your practice runs start beating times the guild board has kept under glass for thirty years'>>

choices.label: Take the times to Coach Marisela Reyes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='derby day, and the mayor herself waves the flag: the twin-launch dock gleams, the hoops snap their pennants over three rooftops, and Coach Marisela Reyes checks your harness twice without a word; Callie Boone, first on the launch dock crew by her own demand, holds up the release and waits on your call'>>

choices.label: Call your line.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='the twin-launch dock gives you speed to spend and one decision left to make in the air's own language: fly the fast line, low under the chimneys where the record lives and the gusts bite between the buildings; or fly the high line over the weathervanes where no junior has hunted, riding whatever the morning sky is hiding up there'>>

choices.label: Fly the fast line under the chimneys.
Fly the high line over the weathervanes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=84 beats='you call the fast line and Callie Boone's release is perfect: the short sprint kayak drops into the chimney canyon flat-out, hoops flicking past like page turns, the whole town's rooftops unrolling a foot below her keel'>>

choices.label: Cross the last hoop.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='you call the high line and climb; over the weathervanes the morning has been keeping a secret, a smooth river of rising air no chart on the guild wall has ever shown, and the short sprint kayak settles into it like a leaf onto a stream'>>

choices.label: Ride the secret river.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the river of warm air carries the short sprint kayak over the last hoop, over the landing lawn, over hedges and one very surprised picnic, while far behind you the derby crowd cranes and points at a glider doing what no chart on the guild wall says a glider should'>>

choices.label: Land where the river ends.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the den is a small warm city of twelve guild pigeons, route maps pinned three deep, and one wall of brass leg-capsules polished like trophies; at the center hangs the post board with Torrent's record, seven summers, never beaten home, and Callie Boone dusts that corner of the board personally'>>

choices.label: Meet your own bird.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Coach Marisela Reyes hands you Skipper, a small slate-grey hen with quick eyes who circles the den twice before every landing, deciding; Callie Boone laughs out loud at her, calls her the ditherer; Coach Marisela Reyes waits until Callie Boone is gone and says quietly that the circling is not dithering, it is thinking, and thinking birds last'>>

choices.label: Start Skipper on the routes.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='the Post Trials close the summer, three legs flown for keeps: the rapids sprint leg, short and savage; the river cross, wide and windy; and the flood-season relay leg, home through worsening weather before the last bell, the leg that makes legends; Callie Boone announces to the whole den that Torrent will fly all three, and looks at you when she says it'>>

choices.label: Plan Skipper's training summer.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='your first post week is all routes and rhythms: you walk the three great legs on foot with a map, the rapids sprint leg cobbles, the wide river cross, the storm-bell line over Mill Hill, while Skipper rides your shoulder and marks each landmark with one thoughtful circle overhead; by week's end you know every rooftop, bridge, and bell between the den and the hills, and Skipper knows them one circle better'>>

choices.label: Choose her one great leg.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='a young bird can be made ready for one great leg, not three, and Coach Marisela Reyes makes you choose Skipper's summer: train her for speed, dawn sprints up Baker's Row against the clock until the rapids sprint leg is hers to argue; or train her weather-sense, reading pressure and sky and shelter ledges, aiming her whole young heart at the flood-season relay leg'>>

choices.label: Train Skipper for the rapids sprint leg.
Train Skipper for the flood-season relay leg.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='sprint summer: every dawn you and Skipper work Baker's Row, den to market cross and home, and every dusk you log her times; she is not fast yet, but she is faster than yesterday, every single day, and her two thinking circles before landing get shorter as she gets surer'>>

choices.label: Shave the last seconds.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='the last seconds hide in the waterproof message tube: the regulation brass message waterproof message tube is a small anchor on a small bird, and the den's old-timers whisper about featherweight capsules, paper-thin shells that are strictly borderline under post rules; one sits in your palm now, light as a beech leaf, entirely unweighed by any judge yet'>>

choices.label: Choose Skipper's trial waterproof message tube.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='the featherweight could be the wingbeat between Skipper and Torrent in the rapids sprint leg, and it might pass the judges' scale, and it might not, and you would fly the trials knowing which word you bet on; the regulation waterproof message tube is honest weight, honest time, and trusts everything to the training you and Skipper actually did; she watches you weigh her fates, head tilted'>>

choices.label: Fly the featherweight waterproof message tube in the trials.
Keep the regulation waterproof message tube and trust the training.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='Skipper flies the rapids sprint leg of her young life, first home by a full three wingbeats over Torrent, and the den explodes, and then the judges' scale comes out at the finish table the way it always does at records, and the featherweight waterproof message tube weighs exactly what you feared'>>

choices.label: Stand at the judges' table.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='regulation brass it is, and the last week of training becomes craft instead of tricks: you file the waterproof message tube mount smoother, teach Skipper a tighter line around the chimney at Baker's corner, and shave her landing circles from two to one; what you cannot make lighter you make cleaner'>>

choices.label: Fly the rapids sprint leg.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='storm-school for a small grey hen: you teach Skipper the pressure drop before rain, the shelter ledges on the mill and the church tower, the home line with the wind behind and the wind across; her double circle stops looking like doubt and starts looking like what it is, a bird reading the whole sky before committing'>>

choices.label: Watch the sky with Skipper.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the secret you learn by accident: Torrent, the unbeatable, hates thunder, and Callie Boone has been hiding it for seven summers, flying him early, flying him around weather, guarding the record from the sky itself; you see it in one rehearsal flash, Torrent flinching off his line and Callie Boone's face going carefully blank'>>

choices.label: Say nothing, for now.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='then the ruling: after a near-miss in the great August squall, Coach Marisela Reyes announces the flood-season relay leg will be cancelled as too dangerous, unless someone proves a safe-flight protocol before trials day; the den looks at its boots; the flood-season relay leg is the leg Skipper was born for, and the clock on proving it starts now'>>

choices.label: Take up the protocol problem.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='two ways to save the flood-season relay leg: take Skipper up in the next real squall and prove the protocol solo, one bird, one junior, and the rope-crew rule quietly bent, glory and the fix in one flight; or bring Callie Boone in, pool what your weather-school knows with what her seven summers know, and design the protocol together, sharing the credit and telling her you have seen Torrent flinch'>>

choices.label: Prove the protocol solo in the next squall.
Bring Callie Boone in and design it together.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='the squall comes on a Thursday and you and Skipper go up into it alone, no rope-crew, no spotter, just a junior on the parapet and a small bird reading a big sky; Skipper flies it perfectly, ledge to ledge to home before the first bell stops swinging, and you land in trouble as deep as the weather'>>

choices.label: Answer for the broken rule.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='Coach Marisela Reyes's anger is quiet and worse for it: the buddy rule is written in old rigger's blood, and no protocol is proven by breaking the rule it rides on; Skipper's flight data is good, so good it will be used, and you are grounded from the trials for a fortnight that contains them'>>

choices.label: Watch the trials from the den.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='you tell Callie Boone everything, the protocol problem and the flinch you saw in August, and after one terrible silence she chooses to trust you back; your weather-school and her seven summers turn out to fit like gears: bell timings, shelter ledges, pressure calls, and a two-bird buddy system where a sky-reader leads and a strong flier holds the line'>>

choices.label: Build the buddy protocol.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the guild adopts the protocol in a week: the flood-season relay leg is saved, the trials confirmed, and Skipper and Torrent become the den's first official buddy pair, the young hen teaching the old champion her double circle while he teaches her to punch a crosswind; Callie Boone starts eating lunch at your bench like it has always been that way'>>

choices.label: Train the first buddy pair.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='trials day saves its truth for the third leg: real weather walking in off the hills, pressure falling, the storm-bell rigged to ring; the postmaster rules the leg flies under your buddy protocol, two birds together, one leading, and leaves the naming of the lead to the two juniors who wrote it; Callie Boone looks at you and does not say Torrent's name'>>

choices.label: Name the lead bird.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='one storm leg, three ways to fly it: Skipper leads, the sky-reader out front with Torrent's strength holding her flank, the protocol flown exactly as designed; Torrent leads, the old champion given his storm at last with Skipper steadying the line behind, and Callie Boone's seven summers honored in front of the whole guild; or jump the postmaster's bell and give the launch word early, ahead of the printed weather schedule your own protocol swears by, to chase the record crossing the den has whispered about all week'>>

choices.label: Let Skipper lead the storm leg, Torrent flanking.
Let Torrent lead, with Skipper holding the line.
Launch early and chase the record crossing.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='Skipper goes up first into the greening sky with Torrent half a wingbeat back and the whole guild on the parapet under ropes and rules; her double circle at the mill ledge reads the squall's one safe seam, and she takes it, and Torrent follows her through'>>

choices.label: Bring them home to the bell.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='Torrent goes up leading with Skipper glued to his flank, and the old champion flies his forbidden weather at last, thunder and all, steadied every time he flinches by a small grey hen holding the line a wingbeat behind; Callie Boone watches her bird's whole secret become his finest flight'>>

choices.label: Meet them at the den door.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='you give the launch word two minutes before the postmaster's bell, chasing a record, and the pair goes up into the very sky the printed schedule was written to avoid; the squall's worst seam arrives exactly where your own timetable said it would, and Skipper's double circle reads it and takes both birds to shelter on the mill ledge, protocol-perfect, waiting out the weather you launched them into'>>

choices.label: Wait at the den rail for the sky to clear.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=failure words=112 beats='the judges are kind and the rules are rules: no second hoop, no derby seed, and the re-fly they grant you lands a month after summer ends; Coach Marisela Reyes walks you home past the awning and says the only thing he ever says twice, big wings are crewed wings; the last weeks become crewed practice with Callie Boone on the tail line, and the autumn re-fly goes clean through all three hoops with only the crew and the pigeons watching, which turns out to be plenty'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=116 beats='the derby comes and the long touring canoe flies it mid-pack, steady as a ferry, and it does not matter at all: by then half the field launches with your crew calls and flies with your trim tabs, and every clean turn through the hoops is a little bit yours; Coach Marisela Reyes enters the tab design and the tail-line drill in the guild book with your name in rigger's capitals and the title trim doctor underneath; you came to win a derby and instead discovered the quiet trade of making other people's wings honest, which Coach Marisela Reyes says was always the better secret'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=118 beats='three clean hoops and the fastest junior time of the day plants you second overall behind a guild senior twice your size and years, and first junior on the derby board in paint that Coach Marisela Reyes lets you stir; Callie Boone's rebuilt entry takes third and she splits her victory toffee with you on the parapet, co-plotting openly for next summer; the trainer that came to you half-wrecked goes into winter storage with a ribbon on her keel'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=112 beats='the flood-season relay leg flies on trials day under a protocol built from Skipper's Thursday, adopted by the guild without your name on it, that being the price Coach Marisela Reyes names for the manner of its proving; you watch from the den rail with Skipper on your shoulder while other birds fly her route; in September the postmaster quietly adds a line to the protocol sheet, first flown by Skipper, and Coach Marisela Reyes lets it stand, rules, he says, are owed to the living, credit to the honest'>>
