<<FILL role=completion words=118 beats='they come home together on the bell, Torrent leading brave and Skipper precise, and Callie Boone says thank you out loud in front of the entire den, which costs her more than the record ever did; the post board gets a new line under Torrent's seven summers, storm-flown at last, and the den gets co-captains, one of whom is you; rivals, the postmaster observes, make middling stories, but a champion who learned to be caught, that one he will tell for years'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=116 beats='the birds sit out the worst of the squall on the mill ledge, dry and thoroughly unimpressed, and glide home together long after the last bell has stopped swinging: no record, no scored crossing, two safe birds, and one junior who jumped a bell that existed for a reason; the postmaster reads you the schedule's small print, which turns out to be in Coach Marisela Reyes's handwriting, and Coach Marisela Reyes adds the lesson underneath, the timing was the invention; Callie Boone flies the make-up crossing with you a week later, by the book, and the book, you admit out loud, flies better'>>

<<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the gadget shop is the guild's front tooth, opening right onto Trading Post Lane: a wall of little labeled drawers, hooks of half-finished helpers, and Nurse Aiyana Cole in an apron of forty pockets; her first law goes up over your bench in her own handwriting, ask the shopkeeper twice, build once'>>

choices.label: Walk Trading Post Lane with Nurse Aiyana Cole.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=102 beats='the street is a parade of solvable troubles: Cook Delia Ortiz the baker fights his dawn rush up a narrow stair, forty loaves at a time; Ranger Sam Whitfield's beautiful tilted apple display sheds apples down the cobbles every time the tram rumbles past; and at summer's end the Camp Showcase takes over the whole street, ribbons and all, judged on helpers that truly help'>>

choices.label: Meet the other junior.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='Theo Alvarez's bench is a small museum: labeled tools, squared drawings, edges you could shave by; she has entered the fair twice and never won, and her handshake says she does not intend to lose to the new junior; Nurse Aiyana Cole posts the summer ledger, every inventor adopts one shopkeeper, and your fair entry must grow out of that real work'>>

choices.label: Choose your shopkeeper.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='two names are left unclaimed in the ledger and Nurse Aiyana Cole holds the pen out to you: Cook Delia Ortiz, whose dawn rush is a four-in-the-morning war between forty loaves, one narrow stair, and a bell that never rings on time; or Ranger Sam Whitfield, whose runaway apples have rolled so far down Trading Post Lane that the fishmonger catches them in his hat; Theo Alvarez signs for the fishmonger without looking up'>>

choices.label: Adopt Cook Delia Ortiz the baker.
Adopt Ranger Sam Whitfield the greengrocer.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='four in the morning at Cook Delia Ortiz's bakery is weather of its own, flour fog and oven heat: you watch him haul basket after basket up the narrow stair while eight ovens tick to eight different clocks in his head, and you fill three notebook pages before the sun is even up'>>

choices.label: Ask him what the problem is.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=104 beats='asked once, Cook Delia Ortiz says the stair is the problem; asked twice, Nurse Aiyana Cole's rule working like a charm, he admits the truth: it is the timing, eight ovens proofing and baking on eight schedules with nothing but his memory; back at the bench your first mess-hall proofing timer sketch collects one pointed correction from Theo Alvarez in passing, your return springs will tire by August, which is irritating and, you realize by supper, correct'>>

choices.label: Weigh the two builds at the bench.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='two builds could change Cook Delia Ortiz's dawn and there is summer enough for one done well: the mess-hall proofing timer with eight weighted flags, springless thanks to Theo Alvarez's unwanted advice, the fix for the real problem he only admitted when asked twice; or a supply hoist basket lift up the narrow stair, counterweights and pulleys from Nurse Aiyana Cole's crates, the fix for the problem everyone can see'>>

choices.label: Build the mess-hall proofing timer with weighted flags.
Build the supply hoist for the narrow stair.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='mess-hall proofing timer version one is cuckoo chaos: flags drop in pairs, flour packs the works, and one morning the whole dial spins backward to Cook Delia Ortiz's enormous delight; you learn to case the works against flour the way Nurse Aiyana Cole cases everything on this street, twice'>>

choices.label: Build version two.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='version two, weighted flags riding sealed brass tubes, runs two ovens for a full week without a miss, and Cook Delia Ortiz stops checking those two ovens at all, which he has not done in thirty years; six ovens to go, and the fair is suddenly close enough to smell the bunting'>>

choices.label: Face the fair deadline.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='the fair asks its question: scale version two up to all eight ovens in one heroic fortnight, a full bakery clock on the fair stage with Cook Delia Ortiz himself demonstrating, glory or tangle; or perfect the two-oven clock you know is true, enter it small and finished, and let eight ovens wait for autumn; Nurse Aiyana Cole, asked for advice, taps her own first law and says nothing'>>

choices.label: Perfect the two-oven clock and enter it small.
Scale to all eight ovens before the fair.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='you spend the fortnight making the small clock unarguable: sealed tubes polished, flags balanced to drop in half a heartbeat, and a little brass door for cleaning out flour that Nurse Aiyana Cole inspects twice and pronounces sensible, which from Nurse Aiyana Cole is a parade'>>

choices.label: Fit it at the bakery first.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the last days go to ovens three and four at the bakery itself, the clock earning its keep in real flour weather while Cook Delia Ortiz learns to trust it, plus a hand-lettered card explaining the whole trick; it is the least spectacular entry on your street of the fair, and the most finished thing you have ever made'>>

choices.label: Show it at the fair.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the eight-flag clock is magnificent for exactly four minutes of fair morning, then flag six snags flag five, the tangle walks across the dial like knitting coming undone, and somewhere behind the stage Cook Delia Ortiz's actual bread, on the old system of his memory, quietly over-bakes'>>

choices.label: Untangle in front of the judges.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the supply hoist starts as chalk lines on the bakery wall: a basket sized to Cook Delia Ortiz's trays, counterweights from Nurse Aiyana Cole's crates, pulleys trued on your own bench; the narrow stair measures narrower every visit, and the basket clears it by the width of two fingers, if nothing swings'>>

choices.label: Rig the first test lift.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the test lift glides up like a dream and comes down like an anvil: a loaded basket needs a brake, a real one, and the finest brake design in the guild lives in Theo Alvarez's locked drawer, built for the fair entry she guards like a dragon; she catches you looking at the drawer and slides it shut with her elbow'>>

choices.label: Solve the brake problem.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='an old millwright's book in the guild library shows a rope-twist brake you could improvise by Friday, diagrams smudged, margins full of some dead tinkerer's warnings; or there is the door you have not tried, offering Theo Alvarez a straight trade, your clean gear-cutting, which she has watched sideways for weeks, for her brake; the basket waits at the top of the stair, wedged'>>

choices.label: Offer Theo Alvarez a trade for her brake design.
Improvise the rope-twist brake from the book.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='the trade lands: your gear-cutting for her brake, working side by side at her spotless bench, where you learn she checks everything three times because her first fair entry failed in front of everyone; her brake plus your gears makes the lift glide up and settle down soft as a yawn, fully loaded'>>

choices.label: Show Cook Delia Ortiz the finished lift.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='triumph lasts one day: the health inspector, making fair-season rounds, rules that no rope lift may run beside food without a guard cage, and posts the notice with three days left before the fair; Cook Delia Ortiz offers to hide the lift till September, and Theo Alvarez surprises you both by saying no, we fix it'>>

choices.label: Design the guard cage.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='one long evening in Nurse Aiyana Cole's mesh drawers turns up the answer: a folding cage of baker's cooling mesh that latches shut around the basket's path and folds flat for cleaning, Theo Alvarez drawing while you cut, Nurse Aiyana Cole appearing at exactly the right moments with exactly the right hinge, the way she always somehow does'>>

choices.label: Bolt the cage in before dawn.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='you bolt the caged lift into the bakery in the dark, and dawn arrives like a held breath: baskets rise, the brake sighs, the inspector initials the cage latch, and Cook Delia Ortiz walks up his own stair empty-handed for the first time in thirty years, laughing all the way'>>

choices.label: Let the week tell the story.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='word travels the way warm bread does: the dawn rush halves, the extra hour becomes a new seeded loaf named after you, and on judging day the fair judges leave their tent and walk to the bakery to watch the helper actually helping, which none of them can remember doing before'>>

choices.label: Meet the judges at the bakery.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='the rope-twist brake judders like a nervous laugh, holds through every empty test, and then meets its first real load of morning rolls; the basket sails down the stairwell in stately slow disaster and forty rolls take Trading Post Lane at a gallop, pursued by pigeons'>>

choices.label: Chase the rolls down the street.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='nobody is hurt and the ducks in the fountain eat like kings, but Cook Delia Ortiz retires the lift on the spot until it earns a proper brake, and he is right; that evening you find Theo Alvarez's brake drawings on your bench, unasked, weighted down with one of her perfect gears and no note at all'>>

choices.label: Read the drawings she left.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='Ranger Sam Whitfield's stall is the prettiest physics problem on Trading Post Lane: a tilted display that shows every apple to the sun, and every time the tram rumbles past, the top row breaks loose and rolls for the harbor; she catches two, the fishmonger's hat catches one, and Trading Post Lane eats the rest; she has lost a crate a week all spring'>>

choices.label: Ask her once, then twice.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='asked once, she says stop the apples; asked twice, she stops weighing and tells the truth of her trade: the tilt is the shop, people buy apples they can see blushing, and every flat safe tray she has tried sold half; whatever you build must keep the tilt and keep the apples, both, or it is no helper at all'>>

choices.label: Take the problem to the bench.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='back at the shop, Nurse Aiyana Cole unveils the fair's new crown: Trading Post Row, a judged morning where every helper runs live at its own real stall, the whole street working at once; Theo Alvarez is deep in a descaling machine for the fishmonger, muttering at fish grit, and the row will put your stall and hers side by side on fair morning, working or not'>>

choices.label: Learn the stall before you build.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='you spend the week measuring instead of making: the tilt's exact angle, the top row's tipping point, the tram timetable copied into your notebook, and a chalk mark on the cobbles where the rumble hits hardest; Nurse Aiyana Cole calls it the most invisible work an inventor does and the only kind that never gets rebuilt'>>

choices.label: Choose your line of attack.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=104 beats='two inventions could save the apples and there is summer enough to master one: a vibration cradle under the stall's legs, springs and sand-pots tuned to drink the tram's shudder before it ever reaches the fruit; or an berry-catcher rail along the display's lip, with a felt-lined return chute that rolls runaways gently home to the bottom of the tilt; the tram bell rings the quarter hour like a taunt'>>

choices.label: Build the vibration cradle for the stall's legs.
Build the berry-catcher rail and return chute.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='vibration cradle school: you build spring-and-sand-pot feet on the workshop floor and shake the bench to the tram timetable, tuning each pot until a rumble goes in and a shiver comes out; on the floor of the shop it works every time, and the floor of the shop, Nurse Aiyana Cole observes, is not Trading Post Lane'>>

choices.label: Plan the street test.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the cobbles are the true exam: the same vibration cradle that sits stone-calm on the workshop floor shivers differently on stone, Nurse Aiyana Cole warns, and only a real tram on the real street will tell you which sand-pot is lying; street testing, though, is a permitted thing, with a form and a stamp and a clerk who cannot be hurried'>>

choices.label: Ask the clerk about a permit.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='the clerk's calendar says one week for the stamp, which leaves two bare days between permit and Trading Post Row, enough to tune four sand-pots if nothing surprises you; or there is the other way, wheeling the stall out under the gas lamps tonight and testing against the late trams with nobody's leave asked, data by dawn, and the nightwatch walks that beat twice an hour'>>

choices.label: Choose how to get your data.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=106 beats='the night test would settle every spring and sand-pot this week, with days to spare, and the guild's street rights are not yours to gamble, and both of those things are true at once; the permit path is honest and slow and leaves you two bare days of tuning before the row, betting the summer on a week you cannot spend building; the last tram of the night rumbles past the shop window like a dare'>>

choices.label: Wait for the permit and lose the week.
Test on the street tonight, without the permit.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the lost week becomes the found week: while the permit crawls, you park a jar of dried peas on Ranger Sam Whitfield's counter and chart every tram's rumble by how hard the peas dance, morning to closing, until you know the street's shudder better than the tram drivers do; the permit arrives with two days to tune, and your vibration cradle is tuned to data nobody else has'>>

choices.label: Tune the vibration cradle to the chart.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='the vibration cradle drinks its first real tram beautifully at half past eleven, and the nightwatch rounds the corner at twenty to twelve; what follows involves lanterns, a ledger, Nurse Aiyana Cole arriving in her dressing gown to vouch for you, and the guild's street-testing rights suspended for the season by a sergeant who is very sorry and does it anyway'>>

choices.label: Face the shop in the morning.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='the berry-catcher rail rises along the display's lip: a slim brass berry-catcher rail that lets buyers reach in but not apples out, feeding a canvas return chute that rolls escapees home to the bottom of the tilt; version one works perfectly and bruises every apple it saves, which Ranger Sam Whitfield points out makes it a very tidy cider press'>>

choices.label: Cure the bruising.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=96 beats='felt is the cure, glued in strips down the chute until a dropped apple lands like a secret; Ranger Sam Whitfield's daughter tests it by juggling over the display at closing time, and the chute forgives everything she throws; two stalls down, Theo Alvarez's descaling machine jams again on fish-scale grit, and her muttering has gone quiet, which from Theo Alvarez is the bad sign'>>

choices.label: Keep one eye on stall nine.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=98 beats='two weeks to Trading Post Row: your berry-catcher rail is done and drilled, apples rolling home twenty-nine times in thirty, while Theo Alvarez's scaler eats itself daily and she files the same jammed part over and over, too proud to ask the junior who beat her to a finished entry; Nurse Aiyana Cole says nothing at all in a way that is extremely loud'>>

choices.label: Decide what neighbors are for.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=108 beats='the contest math is simple and cold: every hour at Theo Alvarez's stall is an hour your own entry is not being polished, and Trading Post Row hands out exactly one first ribbon; you could spend the last fortnight making the berry-catcher rail unbeatable, drilling the demo until no judge can look away; or you could knock on the bench of the girl who has never once asked for help, carrying your felt scraps and your chute tricks, and find out what happens to contests then'>>

choices.label: Knock on Theo Alvarez's bench and offer your help.
Polish your own entry; the row is a contest.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='you polish until the berry-catcher rail cannot miss: thirty for thirty, then fifty, the felt renewed, the berry-catcher rail buffed blinding, the demo patter tight as a drum; two stalls down the muttering stops one afternoon and does not start again, and you keep your eyes on your own beautiful berry-catcher rail and tell yourself the row is a contest'>>

choices.label: Take a perfect entry to the row.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='Trading Post Row morning: Theo Alvarez's stall stands dark under a folded tarp, withdrawn, the fishmonger back to scaling by hand with a shrug that forgives nobody in particular; your berry-catcher rail runs fifty for fifty past a judges' table that keeps glancing at the gap in the row where a scaler should have been singing'>>

choices.label: Hear the row scored as a row.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=94 beats='Theo Alvarez opens her jammed scaler wordlessly, which is how you know how bad it is: fish-scale grit has eaten the slide your whole chute trick was born to fix, and your felt-and-canvas lining fits her machine like it was always missing; in trade she re-cuts your berry-catcher rail's berry-catcher rail brackets so true the berry-catcher rail rings when you tap it'>>

choices.label: Rehearse Trading Post Row together.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=92 beats='rehearsal morning, the whole street runs at once: apples rolling home, fish scaling clean, Cook Delia Ortiz's bell ringing on time, a dozen small helpers humming at a dozen stalls, and Nurse Aiyana Cole walks the length of Trading Post Row slow as church, saying nothing, smiling like the street is a machine she built herself, which, you realize, it rather is'>>

choices.label: Wake early for fair morning.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=100 beats='fair morning: the mayor at the head of the row with the bell rope in hand, judges clipboards ready, Ranger Sam Whitfield arranging her bravest tilt of the summer; she asks where you will stand when the bell goes, and at the next stall Theo Alvarez tightens her last bolt, looks over at you, and leaves the question sitting in the sun between the two of you'>>

choices.label: Choose your place in the row.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=choice words=112 beats='the bell rope rises and three places call you at once: Theo Alvarez's stall, co-running the scaler you helped save, two names on one machine at the fair that broke her heart twice; your own stall, running the berry-catcher rail for the judges at Ranger Sam Whitfield's side, the summer's work shown by its maker; or the head of the row by the mayor's bell, where every visitor passes first, if you hurry your stall up there now, trading Ranger Sam Whitfield's real corner, tilt, tram, and all, for a borrowed flat table in the best spot on the street'>>

choices.label: Stand with Theo Alvarez and co-run the scaler.
Run the berry-catcher rail at your own stall.
Move your stall to the head of the row.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=88 beats='you take your place at Theo Alvarez's side and feed the scaler its first fair-morning fish as the bell fades; the felt-lined slide sheds grit like rain off canvas, the machine sings the whole morning through, and two stalls down you can hear, every quarter hour, the soft thunder of your own berry-catcher rail rolling apples home to no one'>>

choices.label: Stand back for the scoring.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=86 beats='the bell rings, the first tram of the morning rumbles down Trading Post Lane on schedule, and the judges reach Ranger Sam Whitfield's stall just as the top row of apples breaks loose in the sun exactly the way it has all summer; the berry-catcher rail and the chute do the rest while you narrate nothing at all'>>

choices.label: Count the apples home.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=rising words=90 beats='you talk the row steward into the head-of-row spot and rebuild the display on a borrowed flat table in twenty rushed minutes, skipping the asked-twice question you of all juniors should have asked: prime position, biggest crowds, and a berry-catcher rail guarding apples that, on level boards far from the tram's rumble, have not the slightest intention of rolling anywhere'>>

choices.label: Present the berry-catcher rail to the judges.

nodes.body: <<FILL role=ending words=116 beats='no ribbon, and the right stranger: the flour mill's engineer stands at your small table for twenty entire minutes, works the flags, opens the cleaning door, reads the card twice, and orders three clocks for the mill floor before walking on; your first commission, written on the back of the engineer's calling card; Nurse Aiyana Cole frames the order slip over your bench and tells the whole shop that ribbons fade but repeat customers, dear, are forever'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=112 beats='Cook Delia Ortiz rescues the morning like the showman he secretly is, selling the dark tray as extra-crusty specials to a laughing crowd and feeding the judges the evidence; your demo becomes the honest two-oven clock, which works perfectly and wins polite applause and no ribbon; on the walk home he orders the eight-oven clock anyway, built slow over autumn, one oven a week, and Nurse Aiyana Cole frames your fair-morning to-do list as a lesson titled ambition, with flour in it'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=118 beats='the judges score the lift where it lives, between the flour fog and the cooling racks, and invent a ribbon on the spot, best helper at work, because none of their categories fit a machine that never came to the fair; the breakfast bun with your name sells out by eight for the rest of the summer, and Theo Alvarez's brake gets its own line on the award card at your insistence; Nurse Aiyana Cole adds a second law over your bench, the best booth, dear, is the shop itself'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=114 beats='the lift waits for autumn and the brake it deserves, and your summer becomes the study of stopping: you build Theo Alvarez's brake at quarter scale, then half, filling a notebook with why each part is shaped the way it is, and mail her your gear templates in return; at the fair you demo the day it rained rolls as a cautionary exhibit that draws the biggest laugh of the afternoon, and Nurse Aiyana Cole pins a card to it, every inventor owns one famous morning'>>

<<FILL role=ending words=116 beats='on Trading Post Row morning the stall stands stone-still through six trams while the apples doze in their tilt, and the judges hand your vibration cradle second place in the row; the surprise comes at noon, when a man from the tram company asks to buy not the vibration cradle but the jar-of-peas chart, which maps every soft berry-catcher rail joint on Trading Post Lane; Nurse Aiyana Cole shelves a copy in the guild library under reference, your name on the spine, and teaches you the word that matters, the measuring, dear, was the invention'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=114 beats='the vibration cradle is real and the price was real too: with street rights gone for the season, Trading Post Row judges your invention on a cobble-box replica while Ranger Sam Whitfield's actual stall shivers untested a stone's throw away; honorable mention, with a written note about patience that Nurse Aiyana Cole does not frame; she sets you instead to drafting the guild's first night-testing permit proposal for the town council, because the rule that caught you, she says, is a bad rule badly needed, and fixing rules is inventing too'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=112 beats='your berry-catcher rail takes its stall's points and the row loses the ribbon it wanted: the judges' card praises your berry-catcher rail and notes, in small firm letters, that a helper street helps together, and the grand ribbon goes to a smaller row across town that ran complete; you carry home a certificate and a knot in your stomach that outlasts it, and in September you mail Theo Alvarez your gear templates with a one-line note, next summer, both stalls; her answer is a drawing of your berry-catcher rail, corrected, which is Theo Alvarez for yes'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=118 beats='Theo Alvarez's stall takes first in Trading Post Row and the judges read the card aloud, her name and then, because she wrote it there in her squarest lettering, yours; her thank-you speech is four words long, which for Theo Alvarez is an oration, and Ranger Sam Whitfield reports the berry-catcher rail ran perfectly all morning unattended, which Nurse Aiyana Cole pronounces the highest compliment a helper can earn, working best, dear, when you are busy being elsewhere; the two of you spend the last week of summer drawing machines that need four hands on one page'>>

<<FILL role=completion words=122 beats='forty apples roll home to a cheer that brings the whole row running, and Trading Post Row is scored the way it worked, as one machine the length of a street: the grand ribbon names every helper and every inventor, your berry-catcher rail and Theo Alvarez's scaler two lines apart on the same card, the way Nurse Aiyana Cole always intended; Ranger Sam Whitfield sells out by noon, tilt and all, and gives the berry-catcher rail's first saved apple a shelf of its own; the fair closes with the mayor asking Nurse Aiyana Cole what Trading Post Lane will build next year, and Nurse Aiyana Cole looking down the row at her juniors and saying, ask them twice'>>

<<FILL role=failure words=116 beats='the judges stop, watch your berry-catcher rail guard forty perfectly motionless apples, and move on, because a helper shown away from its trouble has nothing to help with; back at her tram-side corner Ranger Sam Whitfield catches runaways in her apron all morning, kind about it and slower every hour, and the scoring card notes one stall out of place; Nurse Aiyana Cole walks you back down the row at noon, bolts the berry-catcher rail where it belonged, and the first apple it rolls home draws a bigger crowd than the head of the row ever did; next year's ledger entry, in your own writing, says judged where it helps'>>
